steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Write the volume formula for a pyramid with a square base
steps.content: For a pyramid, $V = \frac{1}{3} \cdot \text{(base area)} \cdot \text{height}$. Since the base is a square with side length $s$, the base area is $s^2$. Substituting $V = 180$ cubic inches and height $= 15$ inches: $$180 = \frac{1}{3} \cdot s^2 \cdot 15$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content: Simplify the fraction on the right: $$180 = \frac{15s^2}{3} = 5s^2$$ Divide both sides by 5: $$s^2 = 36$$ Take the positive square root: $$s = 6 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content: The perimeter of a square with side length $s$ is $P = 4s$. Substitute $s = 6$: $$P = 4 \cdot 6 = 24 \text{ inches}$$
